title: Sports like Skiing & Biking + Skiing to the South Pole - Request

transcript.text: Hello, hello, hello. Video request. Andy Sullivan goes, hi there, how are you? I'm good, Andy. Thank you. I hope you're well.
Next video request. So what are your thoughts on sports in general, such as skiing, bike riding, and all that?
Also, have you ever thought about skiing to the South Pole? Why is that? Thank you, other than I say.
What are my thoughts about sports in general, such as skiing, bike riding, and all that?
When I was a kid, I really liked skiing.
And, you know, if I had a family, I would want to go skiing again, you know.
It's fun to do with your friends, you know, like follow each other, launch off the cliffs, and race each other down the moguls.
Although I could never get any of my friends to race me down the moguls. I was good at that.
They wanted to do the, like, powder skiing, which is fun.
But I haven't skied for a while. I haven't, you know, I haven't done that for, because it costs money, and then you have to drive.
And I always feel guilty about driving and burning up all that gas just to go skiing.
When I grew up, actually, I was on the Nordic ski team.
This was in 6th and 7th grade, and then I dropped out because I had new problems.
But my brother was on it for, like, five years.
He got really good. He was, like, one of the best in the nation.
But I got third in the Frisco Gold Rush in junior category one year, which is a pretty big race, 10K.
But, yeah, every day after school, we would go skiing up in Eldora, Nordic skiing.
And there was this huge maze of trails.
And, in fact, I want to do that next winter.
I want to Nordic ski up in Eldora because it's just, like, way out.
It's, like, maze of trails just around lakes and through.
And it's really awesome.
We used to do these, like, interval races and stuff.
And then you can go down really fast, and it feels like you're going faster because you're on Nordic skis.
And so there's less stability.
When I was in college, I got into telemark skiing, which I was really into.
Telemark skiing was just with regular hiking boots because it was just, you know, you can feel the ground more
And all the different undulations and everything.
And I got pretty good at that.
But once I launched it, I did a helicopter off of this big cornice in Wolf Creek Pass
And landed it and just kept skiing, man.
It was this, like, huge cliff.
Fucking awesome.
On my teleskis and my hiking boots.
But I think we were on acid.
No, no, no, that was another time.
But, um, anyways, uh, yeah, but I don't really do all that.
Bike riding is the sport that I like now.
Bike riding and weightlifting.
I like kayaking, but kayaking is a sport that I would, river kayaking, but that's,
And there's some good rivers around here.
There's actually one right in Boulder that makes good kayaking in the spring.
But that's a sport you would do with a friend for safety reasons, but also it's just, you know,
It's not as fun to do it alone.
And I don't know anybody who likes to kayak, so.
I mean, I'll probably keep my river kayaks just in case I might meet anybody who might
Want to go kayaking with me, but, um, and of course I'm going to do my kayak trip, my
Sea kayak trip around the Caribbean later on.

Want.
